Title: EIT-001
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. Engagement partner notified of classification concern.

Title: EIT-001
Notes: Initial review completed. Data handling gap confirmed across 2 sub-engagements. Procedure review initiated.

Title: EIT-001
Notes: NS lead briefed. Updated data handling procedures under review with engagement partner. Target closure March 7.

Title: EIT-002
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. Personnel security file flagged for clearance review.

Title: EIT-002
Notes: Clearance review package submitted to NS lead. HR notified of interim access restrictions.

Title: EIT-002
Notes: Moved to monitoring pending HR decision. No access to classified materials in interim. Check-in due Feb 15.

Title: EIT-003
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. Restricted to NS lead and Managing Partner only. Need-to-know basis.

Title: EIT-003
Notes: Evidence package compiled. External security counsel retained. Team member placed on administrative leave pending investigation.

Title: EIT-003
Notes: Escalated to Managing Partner and Chief Risk Officer. External counsel confirmed foreign influence attempt pattern. Incident classified Confidential.

Title: EIT-003
Notes: Executive briefing materials prepared and distributed. Meeting scheduled Feb 26. Law enforcement notification under consideration.

Title: EIT-004
Notes: Promoted from ServiceNow (ticket INC0024871). Endpoint isolated from network. EDR agent deployed.

Title: EIT-004
Notes: EDR forensic scan complete. Malware variant identified (Lockbit 3.0 precursor). No confirmed encryption. Lateral movement scope: 1 endpoint only.

Title: EIT-005
Notes: Promoted from ServiceNow (ticket INC0024612). VPN gateway anomaly detected. 340 accounts flagged for suspicious authentication patterns.

Title: EIT-005
Notes: Scope confirmed. 340 accounts subject to credential stuffing. Escalated to NIS lead and CISO. Force password reset initiated.

Title: EIT-005
Notes: Force reset complete for all 340 accounts. MFA enforcement policy drafted. Geo-blocking rules applied to 14 source IP ranges.

Title: EIT-005
Notes: Contained. No reactivation attempts detected in 5-day monitoring window. MFA enforcement live. Post-incident review scheduled March 5.

Title: EIT-006
Notes: Promoted from ServiceNow (ticket INC0025100). CVE-2026-0831 identified in middleware version 4.2.1. Vendor patch ETA: 5 business days.

Title: EIT-007
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. SharePoint site with client PII shared externally via anonymous link. Privacy & Technology lead notified.

Title: EIT-007
Notes: External sharing links revoked. 847 files confirmed in exposed site. Scope assessment underway. Legal notified for PIPEDA s.10.1 assessment.

Title: EIT-008
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. Potential PIPEDA reportable breach under assessment. Related to EIT-007 data exposure scope.

Title: EIT-008
Notes: Legal counsel engaged. Data mapping exercise initiated to determine if exposure meets reportable threshold under PIPEDA s.10.1.

Title: EIT-008
Notes: Moved to monitoring. Legal opinion pending. No reportable threshold confirmed yet. Decision required before Feb 28 regulatory deadline.

Title: EIT-009
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. Regulator correspondence received Feb 17 citing non-compliant cookie consent banner on client portal.

Title: EIT-009
Notes: Remediation plan submitted to regulator Feb 22. Consent manager configuration update scheduled March 1. Regulator acknowledged receipt.

Title: EIT-010
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. HR system access log anomaly: 2 employee records accessed by account not authorised for HR data.

Title: EIT-010
Notes: Access log reviewed. Confirmed: 2 records accessed without authorisation on Feb 9. IT forensics engaged. Affected employees notified. PIPEDA breach assessment initiated.

Title: EIT-011
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. Formal complaint received via HR portal. Employment Law team alerted. Media monitoring activated as precaution.

Title: EIT-012
Notes: Promoted from intake queue. Anomalous expense claims identified during routine audit. Ethics Committee informed.

Title: EIT-012
Notes: Forensic accounting review initiated. 18 months of expense records under review. Employee placed on paid leave pending outcome.

Title: EIT-012
Notes: Preliminary findings: $34K in unsupported claims identified. Formal report being drafted for Ethics Committee. Final report due Feb 28.

Title: EIT-013
Notes: Promoted from iSight (case PS-2026-0089). Access control sensor on 18th floor restricted zone logged tailgating event at 07:52 AM.

Title: EIT-013
Notes: CCTV footage reviewed and preserved. Individual identified via face recognition. Badge access audit for floors 18-20 initiated. HR and NS leads notified.

Title: EIT-014
Notes: Promoted from iSight (case PS-2026-0092). Visitor log confirmed guest was unescorted in restricted zone for 14 minutes after meeting concluded.

Title: EIT-014
Notes: Visitor escorted from premises. Badge access reviewed - no data assets accessed. Host employee counselled.

Title: EIT-014
Notes: Contained. No data compromise confirmed. Visitor escort policy reminder sent firm-wide. Incident closed in iSight.

Title: EIT-015
Notes: Promoted from iSight (case PS-2026-0091). Laptop reported stolen from unattended bag at Toronto Pearson airport. Laptop unencrypted status under investigation.

Title: EIT-015
Notes: Remote wipe confirmed executed via Intune at 08:47 AM Feb 13. Encryption status confirmed: BitLocker active at time of theft. Police report filed (Toronto Police case #2026-44821).

Title: EIT-015
Notes: Escalated. Engagement partner notified. Client notification decision pending legal review of contractual obligations. Law firm retained for breach notification assessment.
